plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-380 RP12.P06.M00.S07-RP12.P06.M07.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-380 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP12.P06 foundation slice: the M00 Scope Inventory tail, the full M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition, M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice, M05 Permission And Audit Binding, and M06 Synthetic Fixture Set cycles, and the M07 Test And Golden Case Set head as eight descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H12), Claude review packet (C12), and CP00-381 handoff, without opening billing, proforma, invoice, or write-down runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-380 is a clean, descriptor-only continuation of the established RP12 billing-core CP364–CP379 pattern, and its production_ready claim is honest and supported. The pack does NOT over-claim: manifest/command-evidence report production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:descriptor, status implemented_pending_review, with no_real_data:true and writes_product_state:false. Independent read-only inspection confirms: (1) scope identity (CP00-380, Risk C, 150 units, RP12.P06.M00.S07–RP12.P06.M07.S06, upstream CP00-379, next CP00-381) is consistent across review-baseline, manifest, the live closeout-pack-plan.json entry (matching source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65…), registry BILLING_CORE_CP380_PACK_BINDING, and the contract current_pack deepEqual target; (2) the 150-unit deliverable distribution (implementation 56 / security_audit 35 / ui 28 / claude_review 7 / test 24) and micro-title/micro-phase row counts are consistent everywhere and asserted by both validators.js coverage logic and model.test.js / the rp12 validate script; (3) all seven required public exports exist and are implemented; (4) descriptor-only no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold — freezeCp380Result and freezeCp380Validation force every runtime/write/audit/leak flag to the safe value, and there is no fs/db/network/child_process code in packages/billing/src (grep only matched descriptor flags). Authority boundary is intact: the review request grants only Read/Grep/Glob with read_only:true; the contract, no-write attestation, and descriptor all assert claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false, with human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true. Hermes patterns appear only as the deterministic descriptor review packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_…_no_write, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: the contract's invalid_review_blockers enumerate auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped, disabled-tools, and missing-reference/field evidence; validation-report marks summary_only_review_allowed:false (Risk C with security/audit + review-evidence deliverables) and forbidden_tools_present:false; the readiness auth-preflight produced valid JSON with non-zero stdout and ok:true. The hermes production_ready_candidate:true is a local-validation candidate only and is explicitly distinguished from the closed enterprise-trust claim. The unrelated dirty LDIP invalid-attempt file is correctly classified out-of-scope and not treated as this pack's review evidence. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-380 billing core p06 foundation slice descriptor pack
